topic: Perplexity AI search methodology, answer synthesis patterns

entries.a: Vane is a privacy-focused AI answering engine that runs entirely on your own hardware.

entries.q: What are the key features of Vane?
entries.a: Vane features include support for local LLMs and cloud providers, smart search modes, cited sources, and a private search experience.

entries.q: How does Vane support multiple AI providers?
entries.a: Vane supports all major AI providers, including OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, Google Gemini, and Groq, allowing users to mix and match models based on their needs.

entries.q: What is SearxNG and how is it used in Vane?
entries.a: SearxNG is a search engine used in Vane to provide private web search results. Vane can be run with a bundled SearxNG instance or connected to an existing one.
Vane can be installed using Docker, which is the recommended method, or without Docker by installing dependencies and building the application manually.

entries.q: What are the benefits of using Docker to install Vane?
entries.a: Using Docker to install Vane simplifies the setup process, especially for managing environment variables and dependencies.

entries.q: Can I use Vane with my own SearxNG instance?
entries.a: Yes, Vane can be used with your own SearxNG instance by running the slim version of Vane and configuring the SearxNG API URL.

entries.q: What is the purpose of the Discover feature in Vane?
entries.a: The Discover feature in Vane allows users to browse interesting articles and trending content throughout the day, helping them stay informed without having to search.
